bank.csv.header: CSV columns (header optional) & manual entry use this format:
bank.toast.amountNonZero: Amount must be a non-zero number
banks.addDescription: Add a bank account with a nickname, account number, and currency.
banks.title: Banks
banks.toast.addFailed: Could not add bank (DB not ready)
bom.helperBody: Use this page for product builds and component consumption. Use Landed Cost when you need to absorb freight, customs, or other bulk-buy costs into unit value.
bom.helperTitle: Assembly stays separate from landed cost.
bom.subtitle: Use Assembly for build recipes and stock consumption. Landed-cost allocation now has its own workflow.
bom.title: Assembly
landedCost.enterCosts: Enter at least one landed cost
landedCost.extraCostsHelp: Enter the extra charges in the PO currency. Stock revaluation is posted in base value using the purchase order FX rate.
landedCost.historyAccessDenied: Applied-run history is temporarily unavailable for this company account. Posting still uses company-scoped permissions once landed cost access is available.
landedCost.historyEmpty: No landed cost runs have been applied to this purchase order yet.
landedCost.historyHelp: Every posted landed cost run keeps a PO-level audit trail with the applied and unapplied value split.
landedCost.itemsHelp: Enter the bulk quantity and base unit cost for each line. If you picked a PO, these lines are prefilled from it.
landedCost.loadFailed: Failed to load landed cost workspace
landedCost.migrationHint: Posting is disabled until the landed cost migration is applied. Preview remains available, but inventory revaluation will not post yet.
landedCost.migrationNeeded: Apply the landed cost migration before posting revaluation runs
landedCost.note: This page is intentionally separate from Assembly. Use it to calculate landed unit cost before receiving or repricing stock, not to define build recipes.
landedCost.noReceipts: Receive the purchase order first to generate revaluable stock buckets.
landedCost.notesPlaceholder: Optional note for this landed cost run
landedCost.prefillFailed: Failed to load the purchase order receipts
landedCost.previewEmpty: Add at least one valid item line to see the cost allocation preview.
landedCost.previewNote: The current valuation model is weighted average per stock bucket. Revaluation updates the affected stock level average cost rather than isolated receipt layers.
landedCost.previewOnlyBody: This page calculates landed cost and final unit cost, but it does not automatically revalue inventory or post into Assembly. Use the result as a costing reference until a dedicated posting flow is added.
landedCost.sourceHelp: Pick a purchase order that already has received stock. Landed cost will be allocated across those receipt buckets and revalue the matching on-hand inventory.
landedCost.subtitle: Attach extra freight, customs, and handling to a received purchase order. The posted run revalues on-hand inventory through the same weighted-average stock logic used by PO receipts.
landedCost.summaryHelp: Before posting, review how much value will be applied to current stock and how much remains unapplied because the received units are no longer on hand.
landedCost.title: Landed Cost
cash.title: Cash
cash.toast.adjustmentNoRef: Adjustments (ADJ) must not carry a reference ID.
cash.toast.invalidRefUuid: Provide a valid internal reference ID (UUID) for the selected order.

currency.allowedHelp: Keep enabled codes aligned with the currencies you actually buy, sell, and settle in.
currency.title: Currency & FX
customers.name: Name
customers.title: Customers
daily.desc: Daily totals for the selected window
daily.title: Daily Revenue & COGS
dashboard.actionHelpClear: There are no urgent stock exceptions in the current warehouse view.
dashboard.actionHelpLow: Low stock is ordered by severity so the most urgent gaps surface first.
dashboard.cogsEmpty: No shipped issue movements were found in the selected window.
dashboard.cogsShipments: {count} shipped issue movements contributed to COGS.
dashboard.dailyWindowNote: Daily rows reflect the active dashboard window.
dashboard.executiveHelp: Primary metrics remain compact, while the supporting text clarifies what drives each number.
dashboard.filtersHelp: Adjust the date window and warehouse without losing context. The daily breakdown follows the current window.
dashboard.insightsEmpty: No shipped orders were found in the active window, so the operational margin table is intentionally empty.
dashboard.insightsHelp: Operational revenue is attributed per item using line totals first, then shipment-linked COGS when line detail is missing. This is not a settlement-cleared margin view.
dashboard.inventoryHealthy: No low-stock exceptions in the current view.
dashboard.lowStockClear: Everything in the current view is at or above minimum stock.
dashboard.lowStockHelp: Items shown here are below their configured minimum stock level.
dashboard.marginEmpty: Margin will appear once shipment-linked operational revenue is present in the selected window.
dashboard.marginNegative: COGS is currently higher than operational revenue in the active window.
dashboard.marginPositive: Operational revenue remains ahead of COGS in the active window.
dashboard.recentActivityHelp: The latest warehouse activity helps confirm that the tenant and warehouse context are aligned.
dashboard.revenueEmpty: No shipment-linked order revenue is available in the selected window.
dashboard.revenueOrders: {count} shipped orders contributed to this operational revenue view.
dashboard.subtitle: Operational overview for the active company, time window, and warehouse selection. Revenue and margin remain shipment-linked operational indicators during the transition.
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.topProductsHelp: Use this table to see which shipped products are creating or destroying operational margin in the selected window. It does not imply the related orders are settled.
errors.title: Error
fields.autoCompleteWhenShipped: Auto-complete SO when all lines shipped

items.create.title: New item
items.create.help: Create the stock master record once, then use it in purchasing, sales orders, stock movements, and valuation.
items.empty.body: Create your first stock item to start receiving inventory, issuing stock, and valuing on-hand quantity.
items.empty.title: No items yet
items.fields.baseUom.help: This is the stock unit used for movements, on-hand quantity, valuation, and reorder thresholds.
items.fields.minStock.help: Use the minimum stock level to highlight replenishment risk on dashboards and stock views.
items.fields.name: Name
items.list.help: Items listed here become available to warehouse operations, pricing, purchasing, and sales workflows.
items.list.title: Tracked items
items.placeholder.name: Name
items.title: Items
items.subtitle: Define the products your business buys, stores, sells, and counts. Each item starts with a base unit of measure and minimum stock rule that drives stock movements, orders, and replenishment visibility.
items.toast.createPermission: Only Operator and above can create items
items.toast.deletePermission: Only Manager and above can delete items
items.toast.minStockInvalid: Minimum stock must be zero or greater
items.toast.required: Name, SKU, and base unit are required
items.toast.skuUnique: SKU must be unique in this company
items.unitsRequired.body: Every stock item needs a base unit of measure. Create or review your units first so stock quantities, costing, and reorder rules stay consistent.
items.unitsRequired.title: Set up units before creating items
kpi.cogs.title: COGS ({days}d)
kpi.grossMargin.title: Gross Margin
kpi.inventoryValue.help: Sum of (qty × avg_cost) across all warehouses.
kpi.inventoryValue.title: Inventory Value
kpi.revenue.help: Shipment-linked SO revenue uses total_amount × FX; if missing, it falls back to the remaining line totals × FX. It is not a cash-settlement measure.
kpi.revenue.title: Operational Revenue ({days}d)
lowStock.title: Low Stock

movements.enterQtyToPreview: Enter a quantity to preview the base-unit impact
movements.failedCreateSO: Failed to create the Sales Order
movements.adjustHint: Correct on-hand stock when the physical count differs from the system.
movements.issueHint: Remove stock for sales, internal use, or write-offs.
movements.logEmpty: No stock movements match the current filters.
movements.pickBinToSee: Pick a bin to see contents
movements.receiveHint: Add stock into a warehouse bin and capture supplier-side cost.
movements.sameSourceDest: Source and destination are the same
movements.searchPlaceholder: Search by item, note, warehouse, or reference
movements.subtitle: Receive, issue, transfer, and adjust stock with full warehouse and bin context. Use issue with an SO reference when the movement should also create a cash-sale trace.
movements.transferHint: Move stock between warehouses or bins without changing ownership.
movements.unitCostRequiredForIncrease: Unit cost is required when increasing on-hand
notifications.digestTime.helper: 24-hour format. We’ll use your timezone below.
notifications.title: Notifications
onboarding.already: Already verified? Refresh this page after clicking the link.
onboarding.notInCompany: Looks like you're not part of a company yet. Create one to get started.
onboarding.verifyDesc: We sent a verification link to {email}. Open it on the same device/browser to finish sign-in.
orders.addOneLine: Add at least one valid line
orders.insufficientStockBin: Insufficient stock in bin for item {sku}
orders.noConversion: No conversion from {from} to base for {sku}
orders.poApproveSoftFail: Could not set an approved-like status. Left as is.
orders.poCancelSoftFail: Could not set a cancelled/canceled status. Left as is.
orders.poDetailsDesc: Review, select destination bin, and receive
orders.poPrint.title: Purchase Order
orders.confirmedReady: {count} approved workflows are ready for fulfilment
orders.defaultIssueWarehouseHelp: New allocation rows start from this warehouse, but each row can still be changed before posting.
orders.paymentTermsEmptyHelp: This company has no reusable payment terms yet. You can keep the field blank for now.
orders.issueAllocationHelp: Split each issue across the warehouse bins that actually hold stock. Quantities are validated against live on-hand balances before posting.
orders.confirmBeforeIssue: Confirm the sales order before issuing stock.
orders.approveBeforeIssueSales: Approve the sales order before issuing stock.
orders.issueQtyRequired: Enter at least one quantity to issue.
orders.salesApprovalPending: {count} sales orders are awaiting approval and {drafts} drafts still need review.
orders.itemMissingForIssue: Item setup is incomplete for this line.
orders.overAllocateIssue: Allocated quantity exceeds the remaining quantity to issue.
orders.sourceUnavailable: One of the selected stock sources is no longer available.
orders.overAllocateBin: Allocated quantity exceeds what is available in the selected bin.
orders.shippedBrowserDesc: Search, filter and print shipped/closed orders
orders.soDetailsDesc: Review, select source bin, and ship
orders.soPrint.title: Sales Order
orders.title: Orders
orders.uomLoadFailed: Could not load UoM conversions; conversions will be limited.
orders.vendorBillDraftAlreadyExists: A vendor bill draft already exists for this purchase order.
orders.vendorBillAlreadyLinked: A vendor bill is already linked to this purchase order.
orders.vendorBillPermissionRequired: Only users who can prepare finance drafts may raise vendor bills.
orders.vendorBillCancelledBlocked: Cancelled purchase orders cannot raise vendor bills.
orders.vendorBillApprovalRequired: Approve the purchase order before raising a vendor bill.
orders.vendorBillNoBillableLines: This purchase order has no positive-quantity, positive-value lines to bill.
orders.vendorBillReady: Ready to raise a vendor bill from {count} billable line(s).
orders.counterpartyDetailsHelp: These details appear on the document and work for billing, delivery, and service-location scenarios.
orders.deliveryTermsPlaceholder: Delivery, collection, on-site, remote service, etc.
orders.deliveryTermsPlaceholderPO: Incoterms, supplier delivery mode, collection, etc.
orders.documentDetailsHelp: Update commercial terms, customer-facing notes, and document sign-off names without touching line totals or fulfilment logic.
orders.internalNotesPlaceholder: Internal remarks for operations or finance. This stays off the printed document.
orders.lineDescriptionPlaceholder: Optional line description for service scope, specifications, or deliverables
orders.linesHelp: Use the description field for service scope, project detail, or product specifics. Quantity and UoM still support stock and non-stock work.
orders.notesAndInstructionsHelp: Use notes for supplier-facing instructions and internal notes for procurement or receiving remarks that should stay off the printed document.
orders.notesPlaceholder: Visible on the customer-facing document. Use this for scope, delivery notes, or service details.
orders.paymentTermsHelpPurchase: Defaults from the selected supplier and can still be changed here.
orders.paymentTermsHelpSales: Defaults from the selected customer and can still be changed here.
orders.paymentTermsLegacyHelp: Current saved terms: {terms}. Choose a standard term to replace it.
orders.preparedByAutoHelp: Auto-filled from the user who creates the order.
orders.purchaseDocumentDetailsHelp: Update supplier-facing notes and receiving sign-off details without changing quantities, costs, or receiving logic.
orders.purchaseNotesPlaceholder: Visible on the supplier-facing document. Use this for delivery instructions or procurement scope.
orders.purchaseResponsibilityHelp: Prepared, approved, and received names keep the PO usable for both procurement and warehouse control.
orders.purchaseSetupHelp: Capture supplier terms, due dates, and sign-off names before you add the received goods or service lines below.
orders.referencePlaceholder: Customer PO, job, or contract reference
orders.referencePlaceholderPO: Supplier quote, tender, or procurement reference
orders.salesCounterpartyHelp: Keep billing, service location, and sign-off names on the order so the document stays usable for stock and service work.
orders.salesSetupHelp: Capture the commercial details first, then add goods or service lines below.
orders.capturedOnConfirm: Captured when the order is confirmed
orders.capturedOnApprove: Captured when the order is approved
settlements.dialogHelp: Post a full or partial settlement, or review prior entries linked to the active settlement anchor. This workspace follows the current source of truth, not a duplicated order/document exposure.
settlements.empty: No settlement anchors match the current filters.
settlements.filtersHelp: Filter by counterparty, anchor type, workflow, anchor date, or due state without leaving the active company context.
settlements.historyEmpty: No settlements have been posted for this anchor yet.
settlements.overdueHelp: Overdue rows are ranked using the due date of the active settlement anchor, whether that anchor is still an order or already a finance document.
settlements.pendingPayHelp: {count} payable anchors are open across purchase orders awaiting booking and posted vendor bills.
settlements.pendingReceiveHelp: {count} receivable anchors are open across sales orders awaiting issue and issued sales invoices.
settlements.payHelp: Payables appear here from approved purchase orders before booking and from posted vendor bills after booking. Once posted, the vendor bill becomes the canonical settlement anchor.
settlements.receiveHelp: Receivables appear here from approved sales orders before issue and from issued sales invoices after issue. Once issued, the invoice becomes the canonical settlement anchor.
settlements.searchPlaceholder: Reference, counterparty, anchor type, or workflow status
settlements.subtitle: Track receivables and payables from the current settlement truth. Orders remain temporary placeholders only until a sales invoice or vendor bill becomes the anchor.
settlements.stateViewsUnavailable: The settlement state views are not available yet. Apply the settlement-anchor migration and refresh this page.
settlements.title: Receivables & Payables
settlements.transitionNote: Approved orders can hold temporary settlement exposure only until the finance document exists. Once a sales invoice is issued or a vendor bill is posted, the finance document becomes the single settlement anchor and prior cash links are reassociated there.
org.noCompany: Join or create a company first
profile.emailHelp: Your sign-in email is managed by authentication and cannot be edited here.
profile.resetPasswordHelp: Use this if you want to reset your password from a fresh email link instead of changing it here.
profile.subtitle: Keep your account identity and recovery details up to date.
profile.title: Profile
recentMovements.title: Recent Movements
reports.agingBinBreakdownHelp: Bin rows help warehouse teams pinpoint exactly where older stock is sitting inside a site.
reports.agingHelp: Use aging buckets to spot old stock value, where it sits, and which warehouses or bins are carrying slow-moving inventory.
reports.agingQtyHelp: On-hand quantity included in the aging analysis.
reports.agingValueHelp: Total value represented across the aging buckets.
reports.agingWarehouseBreakdownHelp: Warehouse rows combine quantity and value by aging bucket so slow-moving stock is easier to isolate.
reports.agingWarehouseHelp: Warehouses carrying stock in the current aging snapshot.
reports.binBreakdownHelp: Use the bin breakdown to see where value is physically sitting inside each warehouse.
reports.binNote: Bin-level valuation depends on movement bin tracking.
reports.binValuationCaptureHelp: Bin valuation is strongest when movements consistently capture source and destination bins.
reports.coverageHelp: Warehouses and bins currently contributing to valuation.
reports.customerCogsHelp: Base-currency cost carried by the filtered customer movement rows.
reports.customerCoverageHelp: Customers contributing to the current operational revenue view.
reports.customerCoverageReportHelp: Customer scope currently in view for this movement report.
reports.customerMovementCountHelp: Movement rows currently matching the selected customer and date filters.
reports.customerMovementsHelp: Review item-level movement and cost history by customer without leaving the reporting workspace.
reports.customers.title: Customer movements
reports.dataSourcesHelp: Connect order and cash/POS sources for the fullest operational revenue view.
reports.noBinValuationRows: No bin-level valuation rows are available for the current company and filters.
reports.noRevenueInPeriod: No operational revenue was recorded in the selected period.
reports.noTurnoverRows: No turnover rows are available for the selected period.
reports.revenueCashMissing: Cash/POS is not connected, so this report is showing operational order revenue only.
reports.revenueHelp: Review which customers contributed operational revenue in the selected period. This view follows the connected order and cash/POS sources and is not settlement-cleared revenue.
reports.revenueOrdersMissing: Orders are not connected, so this report is showing cash/POS revenue only.
reports.revenueSourcesMissing: No revenue sources are connected. Review the product revenue configuration before relying on this operational revenue view.
reports.revenueTotalHelp: Total revenue visible from the connected operational sources. Open balances and settlement timing are not reflected here.
reports.revenueTransitionNote: This transition report reflects connected operational order and cash sources. It does not mean the underlying orders are invoiced, paid, or settled.
reports.subtitle: Review valuation, turnover, operational revenue, and partner movement summaries using the same company and currency context as the rest of the app.
reports.supplierCoverageHelp: Supplier scope currently in view for this movement report.
reports.supplierMovementCountHelp: Movement rows currently matching the selected supplier and date filters.
reports.supplierMovementsHelp: Review purchase-side movement and value by supplier inside the same reporting context used across inventory and orders.
reports.supplierValueHelp: Base-currency value represented by the filtered supplier movement rows.
reports.suppliers.title: Supplier movements
reports.summary.title: Summary
reports.summary.bestWorst.title: Best & worst sellers (by net units)
reports.summary.valuation.title: Valuation by warehouse
reports.summary.movements.title: Movements (in period) — audit trail
reports.summary.movements.noData: No movements in the selected period.
reports.turnoverCogsHelp: Only linked SO issue movements contribute cost in this report.
reports.turnoverCoverageHelp: Items with turnover metrics in the selected period.
reports.turnoverHelp: Review how quickly stock moved, how long it sat on hand, and how much cost was tied to shipped sales.
reports.turnoverMethodHelp: Sold = shipments less SO reversals. COGS = linked SO issue movements only, excluding internal inventory moves.
reports.turnoverUnitsHelp: Shipments less SO reversals in the reporting window.
reports.valuationHelpAsOf: Review warehouse value at the selected period end using the chosen costing method.
reports.valuationHelpCurrent: Review the current valuation position by warehouse and by bin.
reports.valuationWarehouseHelp: Warehouses are sorted by value concentration so the largest inventory positions surface first.
financeDocs.stateViewsUnavailable: The Step 2 finance-document views are not available yet. Apply the Step 2 migration and refresh this page.
financeDocs.linesEmpty: No document lines have been stored for this finance document yet.
financeDocs.salesInvoices.title: Sales Invoices
financeDocs.salesInvoices.subtitle: Issued sales invoices are the legal fiscal truth for Mozambique while sales orders remain operational and commercial documents only.
financeDocs.salesInvoices.listHelp: Search by legal reference, customer, or linked order. Draft invoices are created from sales orders, then issued through the compliance-gated runtime path.
financeDocs.salesInvoices.searchPlacehol: Search internal reference, customer, or order
financeDocs.salesInvoices.emptyBody: Create the first draft from a confirmed sales order, then issue it from the sales invoice detail page.
financeDocs.salesInvoices.notFound: Sales invoice not found for the active company.
financeDocs.vendorBills.title: Vendor Bills
financeDocs.vendorBills.subtitle: Supplier invoice references stay primary in AP-facing work while the Stockwise internal reference remains the audit trail and system lookup key.
financeDocs.vendorBills.listHelp: Search by supplier invoice reference or internal reference. Draft duplicates are warned here before posting blocks them.
financeDocs.vendorBills.searchPlaceholde: Search supplier invoice or internal reference
financeDocs.vendorBills.emptyBody: Vendor bills appear here after approved purchase orders are booked into AP documents.
financeDocs.vendorBills.notFound: Vendor bill not found for the active company.
financeDocs.vendorBills.duplicateHelp: Draft duplicates are allowed for review, but posting must resolve the conflict or keep the existing posted document voided first.
financeDocs.vendorBills.postedHelper: Posted vendor bills are the AP settlement anchor. Supplier credit notes, supplier debit notes, payments, and outstanding exposure all resolve against this document chain instead of the original purchase order.
financeDocs.vendorBills.draftHelper: Draft vendor bills stay editable until posting. Posting transfers settlement truth from the purchase order into the AP document.
financeDocs.vendorBills.apIdentityHelp: The supplier invoice reference is entered from the supplier document. Stockwise keeps a separate internal key for audit trail, search, and linked AP adjustments.
financeDocs.vendorBills.supplierReferenc: Supplier-origin document reference, entered manually and kept visible for AP operations.
financeDocs.vendorBills.internalKeyHelp: System-generated audit key used for lookup, reconciliation, and linked AP adjustments.
financeDocs.vendorBills.legacyInternalKe: Legacy internal key retained for audit continuity. New AP references use clear VB / VCN / VDN prefixes.
financeDocs.vendorBills.settlementHelp: Posted vendor bills remain the AP settlement anchor. Supplier credits reduce the legal liability, supplier debits increase it, and payments reduce the same live document chain.
financeDocs.vendorBills.adjustmentSummar: This vendor bill already has both supplier credit and supplier debit adjustments. The current AP total reflects the full net document chain before payments are deducted.
Supplier debit notes have increased the legal AP amount on this bill. Outstanding liability reflects those posted upward adjustments.
Supplier credit notes have reduced part of this AP document. Outstanding liability reflects the remaining legal amount after credits and payments.
This vendor bill has been fully credited. It no longer carries an open supplier liability.
No AP adjustment documents have changed this vendor bill yet.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditNotesHelp: Use supplier credit notes for reductions, returns, allowances, and other downward AP corrections linked back to the posted vendor bill.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditNotesResol: This vendor bill is already fully credited. No further supplier credit note can be posted against it.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditNotesPoste: Supplier credit notes can only be created from posted vendor bills.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditNotesEmpty: No supplier credit notes have been posted against this vendor bill yet.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitNotesHelp: Use supplier debit notes for additional charges, omitted supplier value, and other upward AP corrections linked back to the posted vendor bill.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitNotesPosted: Supplier debit notes can only be created from posted vendor bills.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitNotesEmpty: No supplier debit notes have been posted against this vendor bill yet.
financeDocs.vendorBills.adjustmentRefere: Enter the supplier note reference if available
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditReasonPlac: Describe why the supplier amount is being reduced
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitReasonPlace: Describe why the supplier amount must increase
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditReasonRequ: An adjustment reason is required before posting the supplier credit note.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitReasonRequi: An adjustment reason is required before posting the supplier debit note.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditSelectionR: Select at least one vendor bill line to credit.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitSelectionRe: Select at least one vendor bill line to debit.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditNoteFailed: Failed to post the supplier credit note
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitNoteFailed: Failed to post the supplier debit note
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditDialogHelp: Choose a full remaining reduction or build a partial supplier credit note from selected vendor-bill lines. The posted vendor bill remains the AP anchor while credited value accumulates against it.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditModeFullHe: Credit every remaining eligible line balance still open on this vendor bill.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditModePartia: Select specific lines, reduce quantities, or enter a smaller supplier credit value for a partial AP adjustment.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditLinesHelp: Use quantity for returned units or enter a smaller net amount for a partial supplier allowance or reduction.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditLinesEmpty: No vendor-bill lines are available for crediting.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditFullPrevie: This action will credit every remaining eligible line balance still open on the posted vendor bill.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditNothingRem: No remaining creditable value is left on this vendor bill.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditLineEmpty: Enter a quantity and/or amount for {description}.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditQtyTooHigh: Credited quantity cannot exceed the remaining quantity on {description}.
financeDocs.vendorBills.creditAmountTooH: Credited amount cannot exceed the remaining value on {description}.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitDialogHelp: Choose a full AP uplift or build a partial supplier debit note from selected vendor-bill lines. The posted vendor bill remains the AP anchor while debited value accumulates against it.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitModeFullHel: Replicate the posted vendor-bill lines as a full upward AP correction when the supplier value was understated as a whole.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitModePartial: Select specific lines, add extra quantity, or enter a value-only increase for additional supplier charges and short-billed value.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitLinesHelp: Use quantity when the supplier value was short-billed, or enter a net amount for a pure value increase linked back to the vendor-bill line.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitLinesEmpty: No vendor-bill lines are available for debit adjustments.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitFullPreview: This action will replicate the posted vendor-bill lines as a full upward AP adjustment tied back to the same supplier document chain.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitNothingAvai: No debitable source lines are stored on this vendor bill.
financeDocs.vendorBills.debitLineEmpty: Enter a quantity and/or amount for {description}.

settings.companyProfile.emailSubjectPref: Used at the start of reminder subjects. Falls back to Trade/Legal/Company name.
settings.companyProfile.logo.helper: Upload your company logo for use on documents.
settings.companyProfile.logoPath.helper: Leave blank to use the uploaded logo above.
settings.companyProfile.preferredLang.he: Used by reminders/digests. If empty, we guess from the company's country.
settings.companyProfile.title: Company Profile
settings.dueReminders.bccHelp: Optional internal email addresses that should receive a blind-copy when customer due reminders are sent.
settings.dueReminders.bcc.helper: BCC recipients for all reminder emails (comma-separated)
settings.dueReminders.hours.helper: Company local time. Minute precision is supported.
settings.dueReminders.invoiceBaseUrl.hel: Legacy fallback base URL for reminder links. Active reminder routing should follow product document logic automatically.
settings.dueReminders.after.help: Use this for overdue follow-ups such as 1 or 3 days after the due date passes.
settings.dueReminders.after.title: After due date
settings.dueReminders.before.help: Use this for early reminders such as 7, 3, or 1 day before the active receivable document is due.
settings.dueReminders.before.title: Before due date
settings.dueReminders.leadDays.helper: Choose when reminders should go out before, on, or after the due date.
settings.dueReminders.leadDays.summary: You can mix early, on-date, and overdue reminders. Click an existing reminder chip to remove it.
settings.dueReminders.leadDays.validatio: Use whole numbers separated by commas, for example 3, 1, 0, -3.
settings.dueReminders.onDue.help: Turn this on if customers should also receive a reminder on the exact due date.
settings.dueReminders.onDue.title: On due date
settings.dueReminders.recipients.helper: Override customer emails (comma-separated). Leave empty to use customer emails.
settings.dueReminders.title: Due reminders
settings.dueReminders.help: Due reminders run on your company timezone, follow the active AR anchor, use billing emails by default, and can add finance inbox recipients or BCC copies when needed.
settings.digest.title: Daily digest
settings.digest.help: The daily digest is queued once per local day after the selected time and is currently delivered by email only.
settings.digest.emailOnly: Email recipients below are the addresses the digest worker will send to when this feature is enabled.
settings.digest.recipientsHelp: Use one or more company email addresses, separated by commas. SMS and WhatsApp delivery are not active for the daily digest yet.
settings.documents.brandPlaceholder: Leave blank to use your company profile name
settings.documents.brandHelp: Display name on printed documents and exports. If left blank, the company profile name is used.
settings.documents.logoHelp: Paste a URL or upload to Supabase Storage. PNG or SVG works best for printed documents and branded exports.
settings.inventory.title: Inventory valuation
settings.inventory.methodHelp: Operational inventory, stock levels, purchase receipts, and landed cost revaluations currently use weighted average costing.
settings.inventory.futureMethodsHelp: FIFO and LIFO are not yet available for live company inventory valuation, so they are not exposed as selectable company options.
settings.readOnly: Read-only: only Owners / Admins / Managers can edit settings.
settings.notInitialized: Settings are not initialized yet. Ask an Owner or Admin to open this page once to create them.
settings.sales.help: Keep only the operational defaults your team actually controls here. Revenue recognition logic stays in product behavior, not company setup.
settings.summary.companyHelp: Maintained from the live company profile used in onboarding, exports, and printed documents.

cash.settlementsHint: Open receivables and payables live in Settlements. Cash stays focused on balances and posted ledger entries.
orders.purchaseBillingAnchorHelp: Purchase orders remain operational until a vendor bill is posted. Once posted, the vendor bill becomes the active AP anchor for liability, adjustments, and settlement.
orders.purchaseAuditHelp: Shows who created, approved, and received this purchase order. Any linked payment activity is shown separately from the operational workflow.
items.edit.title: Update minimum stock
items.edit.help: Only the minimum stock threshold can be edited after item creation. Core item fields stay read-only to preserve stock history and audit consistency.
items.edit.auditNotice: Item name, code, and measurement fields are locked after creation. Update the reorder threshold here when your replenishment policy changes.
items.edit.minStockHelp: Use this threshold to drive replenishment visibility without changing the original item master data.
items.toast.updatePermission: Only Operator and above can update minimum stock
financeDocs.mz.vatExemptionReasonPlaceho: State the Mozambique VAT exemption reason when exempt lines are present
financeDocs.mz.vatExemptionReasonHelp: Required only when the invoice contains exempt VAT lines. The stored wording is frozen on issue and used on the final document output.
financeDocs.mz.originalAmountHelp: Issued invoice total before receipts and credit notes
financeDocs.mz.creditModeFullHelp: Credit every remaining eligible line balance still open on this invoice.
financeDocs.mz.creditModePartialHelp: Select individual lines, reduce quantities, or enter a smaller credit value for a commercial adjustment.
financeDocs.mz.creditSelectionRequired: Select at least one eligible line to credit.
financeDocs.mz.creditReasonRequired: A correction reason is required before issuing the credit note.
financeDocs.mz.creditVatExemptionReasonH: Required only when the selected credit lines are VAT exempt.
financeDocs.mz.creditLineEmpty: Enter a quantity and/or amount for {description}.
financeDocs.mz.creditQtyTooHigh: Credited quantity cannot exceed the remaining quantity on {description}.
financeDocs.mz.creditAmountTooHigh: Credited amount cannot exceed the remaining value on {description}.
financeDocs.mz.creditTaxTooHigh: Credited tax cannot exceed the remaining tax on {description}.
financeDocs.mz.vatExemptionReasonRequire: A VAT exemption reason is required for exempt lines.
financeDocs.mz.creditLinesHelp: Use quantity for returned units or enter a smaller net amount for a partial value adjustment.
financeDocs.mz.creditLinesEmpty: No invoice lines are available for crediting.
financeDocs.mz.creditFullPreview: This action will credit every remaining eligible line balance still open on the original invoice.
financeDocs.mz.creditNothingRemaining: No remaining creditable value is left on this invoice.
financeDocs.approval.requestFailed: Failed to send the document for approval
financeDocs.approval.returnFailed: Failed to return the document to draft
financeDocs.approval.pendingHelp: This draft is locked while it waits for finance approval. Return it to draft before making further edits.
financeDocs.approval.approvedHelp: This draft has finance approval and is locked pending the legal issue or post action.
financeDocs.approval.financeAuthorityReq: Finance authority is required for legal-document issue, post, void, adjustment, and settlement actions.
financeDocs.permissions.noDraftActions: Your role can review this draft but cannot change its approval or legal state.
settlements.financeAuthorityNotice: Settlement history remains visible, but only finance-authority users can post settlement entries from this workspace.
settlements.reconciliationHelp: Current legal equals original minus credits plus debits. Outstanding equals current legal minus actual cash and bank settlement.
settlements.filteredAnchorsCount: {count} active anchors in the current view
settlements.originalAmountHelp: Issued or posted starting amount before adjustments and settlements
settlements.creditedHelp: Reductions from issued or posted credit notes
settlements.debitedHelp: Increases from issued or posted debit notes
settlements.settledAmountHelp: Actual cash and bank settlement only
settlements.adjustmentNote: Legal adjustments from linked notes stay separate from settlement.
settlements.noAdjustments: No document adjustments are active on order-stage anchors.
settlements.dialogBridgeHelp: Current legal = original - credits + debits. Outstanding = current legal - settled. Credits never count as settlement.
cash.financeAuthorityNotice: Only finance-authority users can post settlement-linked cash receipts and payments.
settings.dueReminders.restricted: Only finance-authority users can change due-reminder settings because reminder policy follows the active legal receivable anchor.
financeDocs.audit.chainHelp: See the operational source, the active legal invoice, and every linked credit, debit, and settlement-sensitive adjustment in the same AR chain.
financeDocs.audit.apChainHelp: See the operational source, the active vendor bill, and every linked supplier adjustment in the same AP chain.
financeDocs.audit.rawHelp: Underlying finance-document event rows kept for low-level inspection and troubleshooting.
financeDocs.audit.reasonCodeRequired: Select a structured reason code before issuing the adjustment document.
financeDocs.audit.orderChainHelp: Operational source before the legal receivable moved into the issued invoice.
financeDocs.audit.invoiceChainHelp: This issued invoice is the active AR anchor for settlements, reminders, credits, debits, and residual exposure.
financeDocs.audit.purchaseChainHelp: Operational source before the legal liability moved into the posted vendor bill.
financeDocs.audit.vendorBillChainHelp: This posted vendor bill is the active AP anchor for payments, supplier credit notes, supplier debit notes, and residual liability.
orders.salesBillingAnchorHelp: Sales orders remain operational until a sales invoice is issued. Once issued, the sales invoice becomes the active AR anchor for collectible value, adjustments, and settlement.
